description_long: This is a bright red Fokker Dr.I triplane, a historic World War I fighter aircraft, featuring three stacked wings with scalloped trailing edges and prominent black-and-white Iron Cross insignias on the upper and lower wings as well as the tail. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the rear, with a rounded nose housing a two-bladed wooden propeller and a visible engine cowling. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and a single seat for the pilot. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both painted red and marked with insignia. The landing gear consists of two main wheels mounted on a fixed axle and a small tail skid. The overall finish is matte, with the majority of the surface painted in a vivid red, accented by black and white insignia.
description: A vivid red Fokker Dr.I triplane with three stacked wings, black-and-white Iron Cross insignias, an open cockpit, and fixed landing gear. The aircraft features a wooden propeller and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: Front view showing the triple stacked wings, central propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, wing arrangement, and tail insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the full span of the three wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit placement, wing structure, and landing gear configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and no winglets; wings have scalloped trailing edges.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
